data_in.description: Preprocessed bag-of-fragments corpus (as a Python corpus object or serialized format compatible with MS2LDA.modeling)
Model hyperparameters: number of topics (n_motifs), training iterations (n_iterations), alpha hyperparameter, beta hyperparameter

data_out.description: Discovered Mass2Motifs in JSON format with fragment and neutral-loss probabilities (motifset.json)

domain_knowledge: LDA hyperparameters (alpha controls topic sparsity, beta controls topic-word sparsity) must be tuned for spectral fragmentation patterns; typical alpha is document-length-normalized, beta is uniform or learned.
Mass2Motifs represent latent topics of co-occurring fragment ions and neutral losses; each motif is a probability distribution over all possible m/z values observed in the corpus.
Convergence is typically assessed via the LDA log-likelihood or perplexity on held-out data; models are considered converged when objective stabilizes across iterations.
The bag-of-fragments representation treats each spectrum as an unordered collection of fragment masses and neutral losses, losing spatial/temporal peak information but enabling topic modeling.

Serialization to JSON and pickle ensures portability: pickle preserves the full LDA state for downstream inference, while JSON makes motifsets human-readable and compatible with visualization and MotifDB comparison.

expert_review: Expert review of LDA hyperparameter choices (alpha, beta, number of topics/motifs) for appropriateness to mass spectrometry fragmentation context
Expert review of motif interpretability: do learned motifs correspond to known or chemically plausible fragmentation patterns
Expert review of convergence diagnostics: assess whether LDA inference reached stable solution (perplexity, topic coherence, or equivalent convergence metric)

cmd: import pickle, json, numpy as np; from sklearn.decomposition import LatentDirichletAllocation; import matplotlib.pyplot as plt; corpus = pickle.load(open('{inputs.corpus_obj}', 'rb')); params = json.load(open('{inputs.lda_params}', 'r')); model = LatentDirichletAllocation(n_components=params['n_motifs'], n_iter=params['n_iterations'], alpha=params.get('alpha', 0.1), beta=params.get('beta', 0.01), random_state=42); model.fit(corpus['X']); pickle.dump(model, open('ms2lda.bin', 'wb')); motifs = {str(i): {'fragments': dict(zip(corpus.get('feature_names', [f'frag_{j}' for j in range(corpus['X'].shape[1])]), model.components_[i].tolist()))} for i in range(params['n_motifs'])}; json.dump(motifs, open('motifset.json', 'w')); plt.figure(); plt.plot(model.bound_); plt.xlabel('Iteration'); plt.ylabel('Log-likelihood'); plt.savefig('convergence_curve.png'); plt.close()

Model hyperparameters: number of topics (n_motifs), training iterations (n_iterations), alpha hyperparameter, beta hyperparameter
methodology_summary: Load preprocessed corpus containing bag-of-fragments representations of preprocessed MS/MS spectra
Initialize LDA model with specified topic count (n_motifs), hyperparameters (alpha, beta), and iteration limit (n_iterations)
Train LDA inference on corpus, iteratively updating topic-fragment and spectrum-topic distributions
Monitor and record convergence metrics (log-likelihood or perplexity) across training iterations
Extract learned Mass2Motif distributions (topic-fragment probability maps) from converged model
Serialize trained model state to pickle binary (ms2lda.bin) and motifset to JSON schema (motifset.json)
Validation: convergence curve shows stable or decreasing objective; motifset contains all n_motifs with non-zero fragment probabilities; model and motifset files are non-empty and deserializable

task_description: Train a Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) model on a preprocessed bag-of-fragments corpus to infer Mass2Motifs, then serialize the trained model and discovered motif set to ms2lda.bin and motifset.json respectively.
task_objective: Implement the LDA inference pipeline to train on a bag-of-fragments corpus and serialize the learned model and motif set as ms2lda.bin and motifset.json.
title: Reconstruct the LDA model training step that produces a trained MS2LDA model artifact from bag-of-fragments input

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Trained LDA model binary artifact (ms2lda.bin), Discovered Mass2Motifs in JSON format with fragment and neutral-loss probabilities (motifset.json), Training convergence curve visualization (convergence_curve.png).
workflow_description: 1. Load the preprocessed bag-of-fragments corpus (generated by the Preprocessing module) in the format expected by MS2LDA.modeling. 2. Initialize an LDA model with specified hyperparameters (alpha, beta) and the desired number of topics (n_motifs). 3. Train the LDA model on the corpus for the specified number of iterations (n_iterations), recording convergence metrics. 4. Extract the learned Mass2Motif distributions (topic-fragment associations) from the trained model. 5. Serialize the trained LDA model to ms2lda.bin using Python pickle. 6. Serialize the discovered motifset (Mass2Motifs with fragment/loss probabilities) to motifset.json in the MS2LDA JSON schema. 7. Generate and save the convergence curve (convergence_curve.png) showing LDA objective or likelihood across iterations as a quality assurance artifact.
